response.justification: The candidate demonstrates strong full-stack development skills and experience with modern software practices, but their background is heavily skewed toward web and mobile technologies (TypeScript, Node.js, React) rather than the C++ and industrial automation focus required for this role. While they have some C/C++ experience and exposure to low-latency systems, their recent work does not align closely with the core technical competencies or security expertise specified in the job description.
strengths: Extensive experience with modern software development practices (TDD, CI/CD, agile methodologies) and full-stack development.
Demonstrated leadership in open-source projects (e.g., GISCollective) and mentoring, indicating strong collaboration and technical communication skills.
Exposure to low-latency and event-driven architectures, which are relevant to the role's focus on performance and scalability.
concerns: Limited recent experience with C++ (last notable C++ work was in 2015), which is a core requirement for this role.
Lack of explicit experience with industrial cybersecurity standards (e.g., IEC 62443) or Secure Development Lifecycle (SDL) practices.
Primary expertise lies in web and mobile technologies (JavaScript/TypeScript, Node.js, React), which are not central to this role.
